page_data.title: Sharing Toys
page_data.learning_objective: Understand that toys can be shared kindly with friends.

activity.instruction: Order two sharing pictures, then role-play: You may use it. Thank you. My turn next, please.
activity.evidence: Child sequences sharing and uses one kind phrase.

teacher_prompt.facilitation: Demonstrate passing one toy with two children; do not force sharing of comfort objects.
questions: What can we say when we share?

parent_prompt.conversation: Practise taking turns with one shared household item or game.
parent_prompt.home_activity: Practise taking turns with one shared household item or game.

illustration.scene: Two-picture before/after sequence with one set of blocks and two children; written model phrases in purposeful bubbles.
illustration.focal_point: Two sequence-number circles and oral role-play.
illustration.style: Premium BCube preschool workbook illustration; clean white base; soft pastels; thick rounded outlines.
negative_constraints: No generated or redrawn BCube logo; reserve the approved position for the official vector asset.
No unapproved text, labels, tasks, decorative frames, empty balloons, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
No unexplained blank areas; every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area serves a named learning action.
No frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, unsafe behaviour, inaccessible instructions, anatomy errors, cropped key elements, or developmentally excessive writing.
No mockup, perspective view, page curl, device frame, hands holding the page, or multiple variants.
No more than three speech balloons or long independent writing.
Page-specific: No grabbing, crying as punishment, forced sharing or extra toys that obscure the sequence.

compiled_prompt:
# BCube Production Prompt v3 — CC-NURSERY-V3-P026

## 1. Release metadata

- Prompt ID: CC-NURSERY-V3-P026
- Version: 3.1.0
- Book: Communication Champions
- Level: Nursery (3+)
- Module: Module 3 – Friends & Communication
- Page: 26 of 41
- Exact title: Sharing Toys
- Source: Communication_Champions_Gold_Individually_Rebuilt.xlsx
- Source SHA-256: caecd24a409b4f531b6018b2076d42cc9222af2f198e7c9e5835cde0bc54bfba

## 2. Production command

Create exactly one final, flat, front-facing A4 portrait page for “Sharing Toys”. Follow this deterministic package only. Produce no explanation, alternate, mockup, or additional page.

## 3. Engine order and precedence

1. Publishing Engine
2. Design Engine
3. Visual Grammar Engine
4. Educational Engine
5. Teaching Engine
6. Parent Partnership Engine
7. Illustration Engine
8. Character Engine
9. Quality Assurance and Prompt Compiler

Precedence: approved page instruction > approved book/level rule > global rule. Do not resolve conflicts by guessing.

## 4. Publishing Engine

- A4 portrait, 210 × 297 mm; 3 mm bleed; minimum 10 mm safe margin; 12 mm binding margin; 300 DPI; CMYK-safe; print-ready.
- Use a 20–28 mm header with the exact title “Sharing Toys” centred and the official BCube logo reserved at top left.
- Use a 10–14 mm footer with page number 26 only at bottom right.
- Never generate or redraw the logo; apply the official vector asset during final layout.

## 5. Design and Visual Grammar Engines

- Page type: activity_page. Layout anchor: Large sharing scene plus circle-choice activity.
- Visual/text balance: 70–80% visual and 20–30% text. Use one dominant focal point and a clear title → model → action → response eye path.
- Blank-space rule: Use at most one sentence starter. Maximum two core activities and one optional extension.
- Clean white base, soft BCube pastel accents, thick rounded outlines, large targets, strong contrast, generous spacing, and no visual clutter.

## 6. Educational Engine

- Objective: Understand that toys can be shared kindly with friends.
- Primary activity: Order two sharing pictures, then role-play: You may use it. Thank you. My turn next, please.
- Secondary activity: Two sequence-number circles and oral role-play.
- Observable evidence: Child sequences sharing and uses one kind phrase.
- Developmental response modes: pointing, gesture, one word, short phrase, colouring, tracing, drawing, matching, or adult-supported dictation.

## 7. Teaching Engine

- Facilitation: Demonstrate passing one toy with two children; do not force sharing of comfort objects.
- Model once, invite participation, pause for processing, scaffold through gesture/choice/model language, and affirm effort without shame.
- Teacher–student interaction must be visible, purposeful, inclusive, and connected to the exact learning objective.
- Assessment evidence: Child sequences sharing and uses one kind phrase.

## 8. Parent Partnership Engine

- Home connection: Practise taking turns with one shared household item or game.
- Use common household materials only; do not create a new scored task or require a device, purchase, or printing.

## 9. Illustration and Character Engines

- Composition: Two-picture before/after sequence with one set of blocks and two children; written model phrases in purposeful bubbles.
- Show natural expressions, correct anatomy, inclusive children and families, clear turn-taking, unobstructed activity areas, and editable visible text.
- Star uses the locked bright-yellow rounded five-point identity, expressive face, blue shoes, and small blue cape; Star demonstrates or encourages without completing the child’s answer.

## 10. Locked individual page specification

- Exact child action: Order two sharing pictures, then role-play: You may use it. Thank you. My turn next, please.
- Exact observable evidence: Child sequences sharing and uses one kind phrase.
- Exact teacher move: Demonstrate passing one toy with two children; do not force sharing of comfort objects.
- Exact teacher question: What can we say when we share?
- Exact home connection: Practise taking turns with one shared household item or game.
- Exact illustration scene: Two-picture before/after sequence with one set of blocks and two children; written model phrases in purposeful bubbles.
- Exact visible text: You may use it. Thank you. My turn next, please.
- Exact response space: Two sequence-number circles and oral role-play.
- Page-specific prohibition: No grabbing, crying as punishment, forced sharing or extra toys that obscure the sequence.
- This section overrides any broader category template or generic instruction elsewhere in the package.

## 11. Negative constraints

- No generated or redrawn BCube logo; reserve the approved position for the official vector asset.
- No unapproved text, labels, tasks, decorative frames, empty balloons, watermarks, QR codes, or third-party marks.
- No unexplained blank areas; every box, line, card, balloon, frame, and open area serves a named learning action.
- No frightening imagery, stereotypes, shame, unsafe behaviour, inaccessible instructions, anatomy errors, cropped key elements, or developmentally excessive writing.
- No mockup, perspective view, page curl, device frame, hands holding the page, or multiple variants.
- No more than three speech balloons or long independent writing.

## 12. Gold QA gate and compiler footer

Verify page identity, geometry, bleed, safe zones, logo reservation, exact title, objective alignment, activity load, teacher interaction, parent extension, expected response, character continuity, inclusion, print suitability, prohibited extras, and unresolved variables. Any critical defect blocks release.
